ab: makaya sun a ku ki susuan?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: makaya ken a kivadaq tjanusun?
makaya, namasilevan aken aravac.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: ui, pukaui aken.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: makaya sun a ku ki susuan?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: makaya sun a ku kivadaqan?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: inika makaya, kedri a ku kineljangan.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: pucug sun nu masukincengelj? pasa vaik a ken a kivala tjanusun?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎？我想去拜訪你可以嗎？
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: nganguaq, ku kaljavaw sun i tjumaq.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: pucug sun nu tiyaw? pasa kisusu aken tua patjara tja milimilingan a payuan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎？我想請問你關於排灣族的傳說故事。
en: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Paiwan tribe.

ab: uri sema gadu aken nu tiyaw, inika ken a i tjumaq dri!
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: na seladenga ken tu kemeljang sun aravac tua patjara sinankakudan nua payuan zuku, sa uri pasakivadaq a ken tjanusun.
ch: A:我聽說您對排灣族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Paiwan people, so I would like to ask you something.

ab: kedri a ku kinljangan, ljakua ki ku sipasemalaw a ku kineljang.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: djavadjavai aki makaya ken a paiga tjanusun? ulja sika tjasaceqalja a pu sivecik nu tjaivililj.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎？這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ui, inika makuda.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pai pidanga su cavilj tucu?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: izuanga a sivapuljuq saka alu anga ku cavilj tucu.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: kakedrian anan sun.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: kedri anan a su cavilj.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ini anemanai, ramaljeng anga ken aravac.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: na kemasinu sun?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: na kemasi Pucunug aken.
en: B: I come from Bunraku.

ab: se payuan a su cekelj uta?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是排灣族人嗎？
en: A: Is your wife also a Paiwan?

ab: ini, se Amais timadju.
en: B: No, she’s Amish.

ab: masan pidalj a kai a su kinasaigu a qivu?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: saigu a ken a penayuanan katua kai nia se Amis.
ch: B: 我會說排灣語和阿美語。
en: B: I speak Paiwan and Pangcah.

ab: namasi levan a ken a kisusu tjanusun tucu, pai qaqivu, nanguaq sun a pasemalaw tua patjara sinan palisi tua patjanu qemaljup ui?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎？
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ui nanguaq. Ka maqacuvucuvung anan a ken vaivaik a ken a murisacemel a sema vukid.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常到山林打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: pinakalevan, nanguaq aravac a su sinitulu tucu.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: namasan paqulid angata a ku siniqaqivu, mana sinitjaucikel nua mareka tja vuvu.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: uri tja tjaljumaza anga a tja kaljavar. pinakalevan aravac a su sinitjaucikel.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: inika makuda, tja cusuaw anga nu tjaivililj.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: uri vaik anga ken, ulja tjaliyaw anan a ku ki tjaucikelen nu tjaivililj.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: nanguaq! Galju anga.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
